methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $15.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Applied Intuition
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $15B

private.name: Together AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $8.3B

data.narrative:
Reflection AI, a Series A–stage AI & Software company founded by Misha Laskin and Ioannis Antonoglou, carries a current post-money valuation of $25.0B on $2.0B raised. The valuation assessment relies on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies spanning private and public markets, including 4 direct stage and sector comps (Applied Intuition at $15.0B, Together AI at $8.3B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, and Black Forest Labs at $3.2B) and Palantir (PLTR) as a public benchmark.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $25.0B base, implies a $50.0B valuation. Peer-median methodology yields $7.0B, derived from the median of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables ranging from $3.2B to $15.0B. These methods reflect fundamentally different assumptions about trajectory and peer relevance.

The triangulated analysis produces a wide range: a low of $5.9B (peer-driven floor), a median of $28.5B, and a high of $57.5B (stage-progression ceiling). The current $25.0B valuation sits near the median, between the peer-based floor and the stage-based ceiling. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier in an AI market with variable growth patterns and whether Applied Intuition—at $15.0B—represents a true peer or an outlier within the comp set.
